info.title: Twitter API Service
info.description:
A comprehensive Twitter API service that provides various Twitter operations including:
        
        * **Search Operations**: Advanced search, hashtags, cashtags
        * **Tweet Operations**: Post, like, retweet, quote, bookmark, delete
        * **User Operations**: Batch user information retrieval
        * **Influencer Operations**: Get tweets from top influencers
        
        All endpoints return standardized responses in the format:
        ```json
        {
            "code": 200,
            "msg": "success", 
            "data": {...}
        }
        ```
        
        ## Authentication
        
        This API uses Bearer token authentication. Include your token in the Authorization header:
        ```
        Authorization: Bearer your_token_here
        ```

get.description: Retrieve Twitter authentication cookie using the provided auth token. This endpoint converts an auth token into a usable cookie for Twitter API requests.

200.description: Twitter authentication cookie

get.summary: Get user info by auth token
get.description: Retrieve Twitter user info using the provided auth token.

200.description: Twitter user info

post.description: Perform advanced search on Twitter with multiple search terms and filters, $0.14/1000 tweets. The maximum input quantity is limited by your plan's QPS. To pass larger input quantity limits, upgrade your plan to get higher QPS.

200.description: List of tweets matching search criteria

post.description: Perform advanced Twitter search with cursor-based pagination. Each page returns up to 20 tweets. Pass next_cursor from the previous response to continue. Pricing: $0.14 per 1,000 tweets. The maximum input quantity is limited by your plan's QPS. To pass larger input quantity limits, upgrade your plan to get higher QPS.

200.description: Current page data (up to 20 tweets) and pagination metadata (has_next_page, next_cursor).

post.description:
Deletes one or more tweets from a specified user account. This endpoint supports two modes:

1.  **Single Tweet Deletion**: Provide a `target_id` to delete a specific tweet.
2.  **Bulk Deletion**: Omit the `target_id` to delete **all** tweets from the user's account.

**Warning**: The bulk deletion mode is irreversible and will permanently remove all tweets from the account. Use with extreme caution.

200.description: Success confirmation

post.description: **Recommended** for fetching tweet replies. Uses cursor-based pagination and avoids timeouts on large threads. The first request (omit next_cursor) returns root_tweet plus the first batch of replies. Pass next_cursor from the previous response to fetch the next page until has_next_page is false. Pricing: $0.14 per 1,000 replies.

schema.description: The ID of the tweet to get replies for.

parameters.description: The ID of the tweet to get replies for.

200.description: Current page replies, optional root_tweet on first page, and pagination metadata.

post.description:
Purchase various Twitter engagement services to boost your social media presence.

**Available Services:**
- **Likes**: Increase tweet likes (10-5,000 per order, $1/100)
- **Retweets**: Boost retweets (10-500 per order, $2/100)  
- **Views**: Enhance tweet views (100-9999999 per order, $1/1000)
- **Bookmarks**: Add bookmarks (10-5000 per order, $2/100)
- **Followers**: Gain followers (10-30,000 per order, $2/100)

**How it works:**
1. Submit your order with service type, tweet URL, and quantity
2. Receive an order ID to track your purchase
3. Monitor progress via the status endpoint
4. Services are delivered gradually over time for natural growth

**Important Notes:**
- All services are delivered by real accounts
- Processing time varies by service type and quantity
- Orders cannot be cancelled once processing begins
- Ensure your Twitter account is public for optimal delivery

200.description: Returns order ID and confirmation details for tracking your purchase

get.description:
Check the delivery status and progress of your Twitter engagement order.

**Response Details:**
- **order_id**: Unique identifier for your order
- **start_count**: Initial engagement count when order began
- **fee**: Total cost of the order in USD
- **status**: Current order status (pending, in_progress, completed, failed)

**Order Status Types:**
- `pending`: Order received and queued for processing
- `in_progress`: Services are being delivered
- `completed`: All services have been delivered successfully
- `partial`: Some services delivered (may continue processing)
- `failed`: Order could not be completed (contact support)

**Note**: Orders are processed 24/7 but delivery speed varies by service type and current queue volume.

200.description: Detailed status information including progress, cost, and delivery status

post.description:
**Step 1 of 5** — Create an empty draft and get an `article_id`.

The `article_id` returned here is required by all subsequent steps.

**Full flow:**
1. ➡️ `POST /x/articles/draft` — get `article_id`
2. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/cover` — *(optional)* set cover image
3. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/title` — set title
4. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/content` — set body (Markdown)
5. `POST /x/articles/{article_id}/publish` — publish and get `tweet_id`

> 💡 **Tip:** For full control (e.g. preview before publish, retry individual steps), use the step-by-step flow above. Use `POST /x/articles/publish` only for quick one-shot publishing.

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

put.summary: [Step 2/5] Upload and set article cover image (optional)
put.description:
**Step 2 of 5 (optional)** — Upload a cover image and attach it to the draft.

- `article_id`: obtained from Step 1
- `cover_image`: an `https://` URL or a local file path

This step can be skipped if you don't need a cover image.

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

put.description:
**Step 3 of 5** — Set the title of the draft.

- `article_id`: obtained from Step 1
- `title`: plain text, no Markdown

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

put.description:
**Step 4 of 5** — Set the article body by providing Markdown text.

- `article_id`: obtained from Step 1
- `markdown`: supports headings, bold/italic, lists, code blocks, and inline images (`![alt](url)`)

Inline images are automatically uploaded to X and embedded in the article.

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

post.description:
**Step 5 of 5** — Publish the draft. Returns `article_id` and `tweet_id`.

- `article_id`: obtained from Step 1
- `visibility`: `Public` (default) / `Followers` / `Mentioned`

⚠️ Make sure Steps 3 and 4 are completed before publishing. A draft without a title or content can still be published but will appear empty.

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

post.description:
Publish a complete X Article in a single call. Suitable for simple, one-shot use cases.

> ⚠️ **Recommended: use the step-by-step flow instead.**
> The step-by-step flow gives you full control — preview the draft before publishing,
> retry individual steps on failure, and reuse the same `article_id` across multiple calls.
>
> Step-by-step endpoints:
> 1. `POST /x/articles/draft` → get `article_id`
> 2. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/cover` *(optional)*
> 3. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/title`
> 4. `PUT  /x/articles/{article_id}/content`
> 5. `POST /x/articles/{article_id}/publish` → get `tweet_id`

**Returns** `article_id` and `tweet_id` on success.

200.description: Successful Response

example.msg: Daily tweet limit reached, please try again tomorrow.

NotificationModel.title: NotificationModel
NotificationModel.description:
Attributes
----------
id : :class:`str`
    The unique identifier of the notification.
timestamp_ms : :class:`int`
    The timestamp of the notification in milliseconds.
icon : :class:`dict`
    Dictionary containing icon data for the notification.
message : :class:`str`
    The message text of the notification.
tweet : :class:`.Tweet`
    The tweet associated with the notification.
from_user : :class:`.User`
    The user who triggered the notification.

cashtags.description: A list of cashtags (stock symbols) mentioned in the Tweet

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique identifier for the Tweet (same as tweet_id)

in_reply_to.title: In Reply To
in_reply_to.description: ID of the Tweet this is a reply to

in_reply_to_user_id.title: In Reply To User Id
in_reply_to_user_id.description: ID of the user this is a reply to

in_reply_to_screen_name.title: In Reply To Screen Name
in_reply_to_screen_name.description: Screen name of the user this is a reply to

is_edit_eligible.title: Is Edit Eligible
is_edit_eligible.description: Indicates if the Tweet is eligible for editing

is_quote_status.title: Is Quote Status
is_quote_status.description: Indicates if this Tweet is a quote of another Tweet

quoted_status_id_str.title: Quoted Status Id Str
quoted_status_id_str.description: The unique identifier for the Tweet that this is a quote of

is_translatable.title: Is Translatable
is_translatable.description: Indicates if the Tweet is translatable

lang.title: Lang
lang.description: The BCP 47 language identifier for the Tweet's content

media.title: Media
media.description: A list of media objects (photos, videos) attached to the Tweet

place.title: Place
place.description: The location associated with the Tweet

poll.title: Poll
poll.description: A poll attached to the Tweet

possibly_sensitive.title: Possibly Sensitive
possibly_sensitive.description: Indicates if the Tweet may contain sensitive content

possibly_sensitive_editable.title: Possibly Sensitive Editable
possibly_sensitive_editable.description: Indicates if the sensitive content flag is editable

quote_count.title: Quote Count
quote_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been quoted

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies to this Tweet

reply_count.title: Reply Count
reply_count.description: Number of replies to this Tweet

reply_to.description: The Tweet object this is a reply to

retweet_count.title: Retweet Count
retweet_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been retweeted

retweeted_tweet.description: The original Tweet object if this is a retweet

thumbnail_title.title: Thumbnail Title
thumbnail_title.description: Title for the thumbnail in a card

thumbnail_url.title: Thumbnail Url
thumbnail_url.description: URL for the thumbnail in a card

urls.title: Urls
urls.description: A list of URLs included in the Tweet

user.description: The user who posted the Tweet

view_count.title: View Count
view_count.description: Number of views for the Tweet

target_count.title: Target Count
target_count.description: The target count of items requested, for context

tweet_id.title: Tweet Id
tweet_id.description: The root tweet ID to fetch the thread for

max_items.title: Max Items
max_items.description: Max number of thread tweets to return

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Fields to include in each tweet. If omitted, all fields are returned.

data.title: Data
data.description: Order status details including progress and delivery information

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Account creation date in Twitter format

verification.title: Verification
verification.description: Whether the user account is verified

is_blue_verified.title: Is Blue Verified
is_blue_verified.description: Whether the user has blue verification badge (Twitter Blue/X Premium)

privacy_policy_url.title: Privacy Policy Url
privacy_policy_url.description: Privacy policy or learn more URL

account_based_in.title: Account Based In
account_based_in.description: Country or region where the account is based

location_accurate.title: Location Accurate
location_accurate.description: Whether the location information is accurate

source.title: Source
source.description: Source of account creation (e.g., 'United States App Store')

username_changes_count.title: Username Changes Count
username_changes_count.description: Number of times the username has been changed

override_verified_year.title: Override Verified Year
override_verified_year.description: Override verified year (if applicable, negative values indicate special cases)

verified_since_msec.title: Verified Since Msec
verified_since_msec.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when the account was verified (negative values indicate special cases)

cookie.title: Cookie
cookie.description: The user's Twitter authentication cookie or Twitter auth_token, [how-to-get-twitter-cookie](https://youtube-promotion.pages.dev/how-to-get-twitter-cookie)

username.title: Username
username.description: The username of the user to unfollow.

cookie.title: Cookie
cookie.description: The user's Twitter authentication cookie or Twitter auth_token, [how-to-get-twitter-cookie](https://youtube-promotion.pages.dev/how-to-get-twitter-cookie)

code.title: Code
code.description: The status code of the response.

data.description: A confirmation of the action's success.

createdAtDatetime.title: Createdatdatetime
createdAtDatetime.description: Account creation datetime in ISO format

verified_type.title: Verified Type
verified_type.description: Verification badge type: blue, business, government, etc.

created_at_datetime.description: Account creation datetime in ISO format

can_dm.title: Can Dm
can_dm.description: Whether the user can be direct messaged

can_media_tag.title: Can Media Tag
can_media_tag.description: Whether the user can be tagged in media

default_profile.title: Default Profile

default_profile_image.title: Default Profile Image
default_profile_image.description: Whether the user has a default profile image

description_urls.title: Description Urls
description_urls.description: List of URLs found in the user's description

urls.description: List of URLs found in the user's profile

favourites_count.title: Favourites Count
favourites_count.description: Number of tweets the user has liked

verified_type.description: Verification badge type when not blue-only: blue, business, government, etc.

is_verified.description: True if the account has any verification badge (blue, business, government, or legacy verified)

is_translator.title: Is Translator

listed_count.title: Listed Count

media_count.title: Media Count

profile_banner_url.title: Profile Banner Url

profile_image_url.description: URL of the user's profile image

statuses_count.title: Statuses Count
statuses_count.description: Number of tweets posted by the user

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor

next_cursor.description: Use this cursor to fetch the next page when has_next_page is true.

requested_count.title: Requested Count
requested_count.description: Original count requested by the client.

effective_count.title: Effective Count

parsed_count.title: Parsed Count

total_fetched_count.title: Total Fetched Count
total_fetched_count.description: Total number of tweets fetched across pages.

next_cursor.description: Pass as cursor to fetch the next page when has_next_page is true
